name: Cin7 API
what_it_does: Multi-channel inventory management API for products, orders, warehouses, and purchase orders used by e-commerce businesses, wholesalers, and manufacturers.
use_cases: Syncing product catalog and inventory levels across sales channels
Automating purchase order creation and supplier management
Warehouse management and stock location tracking
Order fulfillment and shipping workflow automation
Inventory reporting and demand forecasting integration
not_for: Startups or solopreneurs — pricing is mid-market focused
Single-channel sellers who don't need multi-warehouse support
Developers wanting a sandbox environment without full account setup
best_when: An agent needs to manage multi-warehouse inventory, purchase orders, and order fulfillment across multiple sales channels simultaneously.
avoid_when: You only need basic inventory counts or are on a budget — simpler tools like Shopify Inventory API suffice.

auth.notes: API key authentication via query parameter or header. Keys are account-level with no granular scoping — all keys have full access. Key rotation requires manual process through the Cin7 dashboard.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starts ~$349/mo for Standard plan; API access included in Business and above tiers
pricing.notes: No public sandbox or free trial for API access. Requires active paid subscription. API access may be restricted to higher-tier plans.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses are inconsistent — some endpoints return HTTP status codes with plain text messages, others return JSON error objects. Documentation gaps mean agents must handle unexpected formats.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys documented. Agents performing write operations must implement their own deduplication logic to prevent duplicate orders or inventory adjustments.
known_agent_gotchas: Race conditions on inventory levels when multiple channels update simultaneously
No sandbox environment — all testing happens against production data
Rate limits are undocumented; agents must implement conservative backoff
API key is account-wide with no scoping — a compromised key has full access
Some endpoints use camelCase, others use snake_case for field names

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. However, API keys have no scope granularity — full account access on every key. No OAuth, no fine-grained permissions. Key management is manual through the UI.
